title: Add install provenance/state tracking (.basicly/state/install.json)
description:
Record installed catalog version + provenance so upgrades can be reasoned about and `basicly check` can report drift (architecture §9). Written by `basicly install` when it materializes the bundled catalog into a consumer repo (skipped in the authoring repo, where core is its own source). Snapshot the on-disk core state right after materialize, so a later hash mismatch means a hand-edit — the provenance basis the core-sync task basicly-zrj.12.2 needs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `basicly install` in a consumer repo writes `.basicly/state/install.json` (path follows the configured core root) recording: schema version, basicly version, install timestamp, and a per-file sha256 map of the managed core as materialized.
- A second install refreshes the timestamp/version and re-snapshots; the file is valid JSON with a stable schema.
- `basicly check` surfaces provenance drift as advisory notes: core files modified or removed relative to the recorded snapshot, and an installed-vs-current basicly version mismatch. Exit code contract of `check` is unchanged by these notes.
- The authoring repo (core == bundled source) writes no state file and reports no provenance notes.
- Unit/integration tests cover: state written on install, re-install refresh, modified-core drift note, and authoring-repo skip.
close_reason: shipped by the harness loop

title: commit-messages CI gate checks only the subject and misses body-trailer bead ids
description:
## Problem

The quality-gates commit-messages job feeds only the commit SUBJECT (git log %s) to beads-commit-msg.py, but the repo convention puts the beads issue id as a trailing BODY line (and dotted child ids like basicly-onb.6.2 cannot appear in the subject per the conventional-commits rules). So every commit with the id in the body fails CI with 'Missing/unknown beads issue id', even though the local commit-msg hooks (which see the full message) pass.

## Acceptance Criteria

- CI validates the full commit message (subject + body), matching the local commit-msg hooks
- A commit with the beads id in a trailing body line passes the CI commit-messages gate
- The previously-failing commit 5b5f3e9 passes under the fixed check
close_reason: Fixed the commit-messages CI gate to validate the full commit message (git log %B) instead of only the subject (%s), matching the local commit-msg hooks. Verified by simulating the fixed loop over the last 6 commits (all pass, including the previously-failing 5b5f3e9).

title: Claude target install: set worktree bg-isolation to none for harness worktrees
description: Claude Code's background-isolation guard (worktree.bgIsolation, default on) forces a background agent to isolate into .claude/worktrees/ via EnterWorktree before editing, which conflicts with the harness's sibling <repo>.worktrees/ isolation (EnterWorktree cannot target a sibling path). To run the harness under Claude Code, bgIsolation must be none because the harness provides isolation itself. Settings precedence VERIFIED (code.claude.com/docs/en/settings): local settings.local.json (personal, gitignored) overrides project settings.json (team-shared, committed) overrides user global. So on install for the Claude target, basicly sets worktree.bgIsolation=none in the COMMITTED .claude/settings.json (team-wide default that ships with the repo); any user can override in their gitignored .claude/settings.local.json. title: drive the checkpoint-gated loop state machine
description:
## Acceptance Criteria

- advances intake to classify to decompose to build to merge to verify to ship to teardown
- enforces the three human checkpoints and the bounded rework loop via the policy engine
- composes the decomposer, worktree lifecycle, verify runner, and serial merge queue
- resumes correctly from the loop state read purely out of br

## Scope

- `src/basicly/loop.py`
- `tests/test_loop.py`
close_reason: Implemented the checkpoint-gated loop state machine (src/basicly/loop.py): a resumable single-step advance() that reads the current phase from br (loop_state, onb.6.1) and dispatches per phase, composing classify (onb.6.2), the decomposer (onb.4), worktree lifecycle (onb.1), the verify runner (onb.2), and the serial merge queue (onb.5), enforcing the three human checkpoints and the bounded rework loop via the policy engine (onb.3). Key correctness property: because the phase is derived from br, every step either blocks or produces a new br signal that moves the derived phase forward, so handlers and derive_phase never disagree and run_until_blocked cannot spin. Leaf types build in their own worktree; features fan out child worktrees and land them via the merge queue. AdvanceResult reports from/to/action/blocked/needs_input; run_until_blocked driver over advance(). 208 tests pass (18 new); dogfooded on a real throwaway feature (intake records type, blocks on classify checkpoint; fresh advance after approval reconstructs classify phase purely from br and blocks needing children) then hard-deleted. 3rd of onb.6's four children.

comments.author: visicni
comments.text:
NEXT-SESSION PLAN (loop state machine, onb.6.3). 3rd of onb.6's four children; READY now (.1 state-model + .2 classify closed). This is the INTEGRATION HUB — it composes every module built so far. THIN CONDUCTOR: the engine advances phases + enforces gates; the AGENT supplies inputs (work type, decomposition plan) and does the actual coding in worktrees. State is read from br every time (resumable); nothing is cached.

MODULE: new src/basicly/loop.py + tests/test_loop.py.

CORE SHAPE — a single-step, resumable transition:
- advance(repo_root, issue_id, *, config=None, work_type=None, children=None) -> AdvanceResult.
- It FIRST reads loop_state.read_node_state(issue_id) to get the current phase (resume point), then dispatches on phase via a HANDLER DICT (phase -> _handler), mirroring cmd_worktree/cmd_policy dispatch so we stay under PLR0911 (<=6 returns) / PLR0913 (<=5 args) — pass agent inputs in a small frozen Inputs dataclass, not as many kwargs.
- AdvanceResult(frozen): from_phase, to_phase (== from_phase when blocked), action ("classified"/"decomposed"/"built"/"merged"/"verified"/"shipped"/"tore-down"/"blocked"/"escalated"), blocked_reason|None, needs_input|None (what the agent/human must supply next). No exceptions for normal blocks — return a blocked result.

PHASE -> ACTION MAPPING (each composes an already-tested module):
- intake: requires work_type; absent -> blocked(needs_input="work_type"); present -> classify.classify(repo,id,work_type) -> to "classify".
- classify: gated by the classify human checkpoint (policy.checkpoint_approved) AND DoR (classify returns can_leave_classify / policy.definition_of_ready). Both true -> to "decompose"; else blocked(needs classify checkpoint approval and/or DoR sections).
- decompose: requires children plan; absent -> blocked(needs_input="children"); present -> decompose.decompose(repo,id,children); then gated by the decompose checkpoint -> to "build".
- build: for each dependency-unblocked child (loop_state.ready_ranked, concurrency-capped by WorktreeConfig), worktree.create(name) and stash the binding via br update --external-ref loop_state.format_worktree_ref(name,branch). Agent builds + verifies each child (that is child-level advance recursion — see design Q3). When children are green -> to "merge".
- merge: merge.merge_queue(repo, [(name,bead)...], config) in the decomposer's serial/topological order; on a node failure the queue already records rework + escalation (policy) -> surface as blocked/escalated.
- verify: verify.run_verify(repo, mode) + verify.report_gate(repo,id,report); gate_status must show the required gate green (policy.gate_status.can_advance) -> to "ship"; fail -> policy.record_rework + should_escalate (bounded n=2, config.max_rework) -> blocked/escalated.
- ship: gated by the ship human checkpoint -> to "teardown".
- teardown: worktree.cleanup for the node's worktrees + br close the issue -> "done".

DESIGN QUESTIONS TO RESOLVE IN-SESSION:
(Q1) Single-step advance() core vs a run() loop. RECOMMEND: advance() is the primitive (resumable, agent/human re-invokes each step); add a thin run_until_blocked(repo, issue_id, inputs) driver that loops advance() until it blocks/checkpoints/finishes. The CLI (onb.6.4) wires both; one-shot mode (arch 12.2) = auto-approve the middle checkpoint in the driver.
(Q2) Where agent inputs live: pass work_type/children into advance() (or an Inputs dataclass); when a phase needs one and it is absent, block and report needs_input. Keeps the engine agent-agnostic (no prompting inside the engine).
(Q3) Fractal recursion (epic->feature->task, arch 12.1/12.2). RECOMMEND scope onb.6.3 to the SINGLE-TRACK linear machine; children advance via the SAME advance() invoked per child by the driver over ready_ranked. Full auto-recursion inside one advance() call is deferred (leave a clear hook + a note); the AC only requires the linear phase machine + checkpoints + rework + resume. Confirm this boundary.

TESTS (tests/test_loop.py): unit-test each transition by monkeypatching the composed callables on the loop module (loop.read_node_state, loop.classify, loop.decompose, loop.worktree, loop.verify, loop.merge, loop.policy) — same fake pattern as test_merge/test_loop_state. Cover: resume-from-each-phase advances correctly; a pending checkpoint blocks (not error); DoR-not-ready blocks the classify->decompose exit; a failed verify records rework and escalates at the cap; a clean happy path walks intake->...->done. Assert on AdvanceResult (from/to/action/blocked_reason), and that the right module was invoked.

DOGFOOD (real br, keep it light — build/merge are already dogfooded in their own modules): create a throwaway feature, advance() it: intake w/ work_type -> classify (real br type recorded), then classify blocked on the pending checkpoint; approve_checkpoint + add AC so DoR passes -> advance to decompose blocked on needs_input="children". Verify the machine advances/blocks against REAL br, then hard-delete the throwaway.

GUARDRAILS (from this session): verify REAL br json field names before asserting (dependency_type vs type bit us); commit SUBJECT = lowercase letters/digits/spaces/hyphens only, NO . / + / _ (bead id trailing in BODY); the commit-messages CI gate now validates the FULL message (fixed in basicly-acx) so a body-trailer id is fine; before `git add`, check `git status` so no file left staged from a prior attempt sneaks in; pre-commit stashes unstaged tracked files, so commit a SELF-CONSISTENT set (don't split a symbol from its importer); structure advance() as a phase->handler dispatch dict to stay under PLR0911/PLR0913.

AFTER .3: onb.6.4 (basicly loop CLI) unblocks (last child) -> then onb.6 closes -> onb.8 (guidance) unblocks. onb.7 (P3 runner adapters) independent.

Installed wheels (no such ancestor) resolve the packaged copy — verified live via uvx from outside any source tree. title: One-command lifecycle: basicly install (init+update unified) and basicly uninstall
description:
Unify install/init/update into a single idempotent converge command so the consumer story is exactly one command for first install AND every upgrade: uvx --from git+https://github.com/niksavis/basicly@<ref> basicly install. Add the missing removal path as basicly uninstall. Supersedes the separate init/update subcommands — unreleased, so they are removed, not deprecated.

Design investigation (2026-07-15): init has no technical reason to be separate; everything it does is already idempotent skip-existing. Real constraints for unification: (a) preserve the authoring-repo guard (core src == dst), (b) never overwrite a user-edited basicly.toml or overlay content, (c) core upgrade overwrite needs provenance hashes to distinguish upstream changes from user hand-edits (install.json, basicly-8fg). Architecture: §9 (redesigned under this epic).

Acceptance criteria:
- A fresh consumer repo needs exactly one command to get working agent files, skills, and installed hooks.
- Running the same command again (same or newer basicly version) converges: core synced, overlay + basicly.toml untouched, artifacts rebuilt.
- basicly uninstall removes everything managed, preserving user content by default.
- init/update no longer exist in the CLI; docs and the dogfood repo are updated.

title: Implement basicly install: idempotent converge (sync core + scaffold + build + skills-build + hooks-build)
description:
One command replaces init + build + skills-build + hooks-build + update. Reuses the existing idempotent building blocks (write-if-changed sync, managed pre-commit block, skip-existing scaffold).

Behavior:
- Fresh repo: materialize bundled core, scaffold .basicly-local/fragments/user/ + basicly.toml, then build, skills-build (default roots), hooks-build (with hook installation).
- Existing repo: converge (core sync per basicly-zrj.14 once that lands; until then materialize-missing), rebuild all artifacts.
- Keep the authoring-repo guard (bundled core src == dst -> leave in place).
- Remove init and update subcommands from the CLI; update README/next-steps text and tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- In a fresh temp repo: a single basicly install produces AGENTS.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, scoped rules, projected skills, wired+installed hooks, basicly.toml, overlay dir.
- Second run reports convergence with no changes; a user-edited basicly.toml and overlay files are never overwritten.
- Authoring-repo (this repo) run leaves the catalog source in place and still rebuilds artifacts.
- basicly --help no longer lists init/update; tests cover fresh, re-run, and authoring-repo cases.

title: Core catalog upgrade sync: overwrite managed core from bundled catalog, prune removed files, protect hand-edits
description:
The missing half of upgrade: `basicly install` must sync `.basicly/core/` to the bundled catalog version, not just materialize missing files. Core is managed/read-only (§4.2), so upstream wins there; the overlay is never touched. Uses the basicly-8fg provenance snapshot to distinguish upstream changes from user hand-edits.

Sync rules per bundled/on-disk/recorded hash triple:
- missing on disk -> write (new file)
- disk == bundled -> unchanged
- disk == recorded, bundled differs -> upstream change -> overwrite
- disk != recorded (or no snapshot) -> hand-edit -> warn + skip, `--force` overwrites
- on disk, not in bundle, disk == recorded -> upstream removed -> delete
- on disk, not in bundle, disk != recorded or unknown origin -> warn + keep (never delete unknown content)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given an older installed core and a newer bundled catalog: changed core files are overwritten, upstream-removed core files are deleted, overlay fragments/overrides and basicly.toml are byte-for-byte untouched.
- A hand-edited core file (hash mismatch vs .basicly/state/install.json) is not silently clobbered: warn and skip by default, `--force` overwrites; both paths tested.
- Files present in core but unknown to both the bundle and the snapshot are kept with a warning, never deleted.
- After sync, install re-snapshots provenance and rebuilds artifacts so generated files match the new core.
- Tests cover: new/changed/removed upstream files, hand-edit skip and --force, unknown-file keep, overlay untouched.

title: Implement basicly uninstall: remove managed core, state, generated artifacts, projected skills, managed hook block
description:
Removal path so basicly can be taken out of a repo as cleanly as it went in (§9). Must refuse to run in the authoring repo (core == bundled source), since that would delete the catalog source itself.

Scope: delete the managed core root, the state dir, the generated files listed in the manifest (and the manifest), projected SKILL.md files carrying the generated marker in the default skill roots, and the managed repo:local block in .pre-commit-config.yaml (deleting the file and uninstalling the git hooks when nothing else remains in it). Clean up directories left empty. Keep the overlay and basicly.toml unless --purge.

## Acceptance Criteria

- After install then uninstall in a temp repo: .basicly/ (core, state, manifest) is gone, generated files listed in the manifest are gone, projected SKILL.md files are gone, and the pre-commit config is gone when it contained only the managed block.
- With a foreign pre-commit hook present, the config file survives with the foreign hook intact and only the managed block removed.
- .basicly-local/ overlay and basicly.toml are preserved by default; --purge removes them too.
- A hand-written SKILL.md without the generated marker is never deleted.
- Running uninstall twice is a no-op with exit 0; running it in the authoring repo refuses with a clear error.
- Tests cover: default, foreign-hooks, --purge, marker-respecting skill removal, idempotent rerun, and the authoring-repo guard.

title: Consumer-repo robustness: fresh repo without beads or Python tooling
description:
Gaps from the 2026-07-15 review that any fresh consumer (first: the terminal repo) hits immediately after install. The harness and hooks currently assume this repo's own environment (beads workspace present, ruff/pyright/pytest on PATH).

Acceptance criteria:
- A repo with no .beads workspace can commit normally after basicly install (gates active, no beads nagging).
- The loop verify gate fails cleanly, never with a traceback, when a configured check command is absent.

title: beads-commit-msg hook: skip cleanly when no .beads workspace exists
description:
validate() currently demands an issue-id-shaped token in every commit message even when known_ids is None (no .beads/issues.jsonl), so hooks-build in a beads-less consumer blocks normal commits.

Acceptance criteria:
- In a repo without .beads/issues.jsonl, a plain conventional-commit message with no issue id passes the hook.
- With a workspace present, behavior is unchanged: an id is required and must exist in issues.jsonl.
- Hook stays standalone and manager-agnostic; install/hooks-build output mentions how to enable beads tracking (br init) instead of failing silently.
- Unit tests cover both paths.

title: verify runner: clean failure on missing check command; consumer-appropriate default checks
description:
verify.py calls subprocess.run without catching FileNotFoundError, so a consumer without pyright (etc.) gets a traceback from the loop verify gate. DEFAULT_CONFIG_TOML also bakes Python-only checks (ruff/ruff-format/pyright/pytest) into every scaffolded basicly.toml.

Acceptance criteria:
- A [[verify.checks]] command not on PATH yields a failed CheckResult with a readable one-line message and exit 1 — no traceback.
- Scaffolded default checks no longer assume Python tooling on every consumer: commented-out examples or stack detection (record the decision on this bead when implementing).
- Tests cover the missing-command path and the scaffold content.

title: worktree provisioning clobbers shared git hooks with worktree venv paths
description:
Found while shipping basicly-zrj.12.1 (2026-07-15): creating a harness worktree runs pre-commit install from the WORKTREE venv, but git worktrees share .git/hooks with the base checkout, so the installed hook shims embed the worktree venv pre-commit path. Worktree teardown deletes that venv, leaving the base checkout with broken hooks (every commit fails with pre-commit not found) until basicly hooks-build is re-run manually.

Acceptance criteria:
- Creating and tearing down a worktree leaves the base checkout hooks working: a commit in the base checkout succeeds immediately after worktree cleanup.
- Either provision installs hooks in a worktree-safe way (e.g. core.hooksPath per worktree, or shims that resolve pre-commit from PATH/repo venv) or teardown reinstalls hooks from the base checkout.
- Regression test covering create -> teardown -> base-checkout hook run.

title: unify projection engines: shared SyncResult, write-if-changed, and check/build report helpers across skills/hooks/init
description: Review finding 9 (remainder after walker dedup in the locator bug): HookSyncResult duplicates SkillSyncResult, three _write_if_changed variants exist (cli, hooks, skills), and cmd_hooks_build/check are copies of cmd_skills_build/check. Extract one SyncResult + write-if-changed + _report_sync/_report_mismatches used by all three engines; align comparison semantics (bytes vs text/CRLF).

Collapsed cmd_skills/hooks build+check onto shared _report_sync/_report_mismatches, preserving exact output+exit codes. Unified comparison contract on byte-exact (CRLF-safe); added tests/test_projection.py with a CRLF regression. Full gate green (ruff/pyright/pytest 275); dogfooded build/check, skills-build/check, hooks-build/check, catalog-lint/verify with zero generated-file churn. Committed 8dfebc1.

comments.author: niksa
comments.text:
>>> START HERE NEXT SESSION <<< (resumable pickup pointer — no prior memory needed)

WHERE WE ARE: main is clean at HEAD 311cf32; 251 tests pass; ruff/pyright/
basicly check/skills-check/catalog-lint all green. The catalog YAML source-format
migration epic (basicly-c1c) and the git-hook-gates umbrella (basicly-ed2) are
both CLOSED. This chore is the top ready P2 and directly cleans up the code that
migration churned (skills/hooks/init projection engines).

THIS TASK — basicly-zrj.4.1 (chore, P2): unify the projection engines. Three
subsystems (skills, hooks, init/catalog) each hand-roll the same "render/copy →
compare → write-if-changed → report written/unchanged/mismatch" logic. Extract
one shared implementation and route all three through it.

CONFIRMED DUPLICATION (as of HEAD 311cf32):
- Result types: src/basicly/hooks.py `HookSyncResult` (line ~38) duplicates
  src/basicly/skills.py `SkillSyncResult` (line ~47) — both are just
  {written: list[Path], unchanged: list[Path]}.
- write-if-changed: src/basicly/hooks.py `_write_if_changed` (~175, takes bytes,
  mutates a result) vs src/basicly/cli.py `_write_if_changed` (~110, takes str,
  returns bool); skills.py `sync_skills` writes inline (a third variant).
- CLI report copies: cli.py cmd_hooks_build (~454)/cmd_hooks_check (~494) are
  near-copies of cmd_skills_build (~552)/cmd_skills_check (~571) — same
  "print written / No files changed / N written, M unchanged" and
  "Stale ... detected / list mismatches / exit 1" shapes.
- check helpers: hooks.check_hooks (~294) and skills.check_synced_skills return
  the same list[tuple[Path, str]] "mismatches" shape; cli renders both the same way.

PLAN:
1. Add a shared module (e.g. src/basicly/projection.py) with:
   - one `SyncResult(written, unchanged)` dataclass,
   - `write_if_changed(path, content)` — DECIDE the comparison semantics up front
     (see gotcha) and use it everywhere,
   - `report_sync(result, repo_root, *, noun)` and
     `report_mismatches(mismatches, repo_root, *, rebuild_hint)` for the CLI.
2. Point skills.py, hooks.py, and the init/build paths at the shared SyncResult +
   write_if_changed; delete HookSyncResult/SkillSyncResult and the per-file
   _write_if_changed variants.
3. Collapse cmd_hooks_build/check and cmd_skills_build/check onto the shared
   report helpers (keep their distinct nouns/hints).

GOTCHA TO RESOLVE FIRST (the crux): comparison semantics differ today — hooks
compares/writes BYTES (CRLF-sensitive, hook scripts) while skills/cli/init use
TEXT (str, newline-normalizing). Unifying naively could flip a hook file's
line-ending handling. Decide one contract (recommend: bytes-exact compare, write
bytes; callers pass encoded content) or keep a text/bytes parameter, and add a
regression test for CRLF so a hook file is not spuriously rewritten.

VERIFY (dogfood this repo): basicly build + check (no diff), skills-build +
skills-check, hooks-build + hooks-check, catalog-lint, and the full
ruff/pyright/pytest gate. Confirm each from its explicit PASS line and exit code
— do not infer success from a truncated tail.

GUARDRAILS: commit SUBJECT = lowercase letters/digits/spaces/hyphens only, NO
'.'/','/punctuation-end; bead id is a TRAILING BODY line (basicly-zrj.4.1). Run
`git status` before `git add` (pre-commit stashes unstaged tracked files, so
commit a self-consistent set). Close the bead folded into the resolving commit.
Note: another agent may be editing quality-gate.fragment.yaml — reconcile with
`br sync --merge` / rebase if there is a collision on .beads or that fragment.

ENTRY COMMANDS:
  git pull --ff-only
  br update basicly-zrj.4.1 --status in_progress --assignee visicni

title: Migrate skill source format from SKILL.md to a non-discoverable format
description: §4.2/§11.7: catalog skills authored as SKILL.md risk double-loading. Decided fix: author skills in a non-SKILL.md format and project to SKILL.md only at target roots.
design: Umbrella for the skills half: 9wl (schema plus loader) and w39 (projector) land together so skills-build never breaks between them. Part of epic basicly-c1c.
acceptance_criteria: Skills authored as skill.yaml and projected to SKILL.md at target roots only; the double-load source is removed; skills-check and full gate pass.
close_reason: Skill source format migrated SKILL.md -> skill.yaml, projected to SKILL.md at target roots only; double-load source removed; skills-check + full gate (241 tests) pass.

title: Post-release distribution backlog (deferred in §11.8-11.9)
description: Captured so nothing is lost; explicitly out of scope for v0.1 per architecture §11.8-11.9.

title: hooks projection rewrites/compares whole pre-commit config instead of its managed block
description: Review findings 1+2: check_hooks compares full file text against a yaml.safe_dump re-render, so any hand-formatted config (including this repo's own dogfooded one — verified, exits 1) is permanently 'stale'; hooks-build round-trips the whole file through safe_load/safe_dump, destroying comments and reformatting foreign blocks, contradicting the 'never clobbered' contract. Fix: compare managed hooks semantically (parse + subset match by id); rewrite the file only when a managed hook is actually missing/wrong; warn when a rewrite normalizes formatting. Also validate hooks.yaml entries with a clear error instead of a bare KeyError.
close_reason: hooks projection now compares/edits only its managed hooks semantically: managed_hook_mismatches() parses the config and subset-matches each managed hook by id (formatting/comments/block-grouping agnostic); sync_hooks rewrites the co-owned file only when a managed hook is missing/wrong, with a CLI note when a rewrite normalizes formatting; invalid YAML raises a clear error; hooks.yaml entries validated (no bare KeyError). Regression tests: dogfood config passes its own gate (was exit 1), comments survive no-op sync, tampered entry detected+repaired. 67 tests pass.

title: init hardcodes .basicly/core and default-config literal duplicates config.py defaults
description: Review findings 3+4+10: cmd_init materializes into a hardcoded .basicly/core while build/hooks-build honor basicly.toml — with a custom core path, init->build silently generates nothing (reproduced). _core_hooks_dir also derives hooks from core_fragments.parent inconsistently with init. Fix: single core_root notion on ProjectPaths used by init and hooks; guard against absolute core paths leaking into committed config; move the scaffolded basicly.toml default into config.py next to the path defaults with a test asserting they agree.
close_reason: cmd_init now materializes into ProjectPaths.core_root (derived once from core_fragments.parent) instead of a hardcoded .basicly/core; _core_hooks_dir uses the same notion and rejects absolute paths (would bake machine-specific paths into the shared pre-commit config). The scaffolded basicly.toml moved to config.DEFAULT_CONFIG_TOML next to the built-in defaults with a test asserting parse-equality. Regression test reproduces the custom-path consumer flow: init -> build -> hooks-build all target the configured root. 70 tests pass.

title: pre-commit entry string is built unquoted — paths with spaces break every commit
description: Review finding 5: _hook_entry f-string concatenates the script path into 'entry' unquoted; pre-commit shlex-splits entry, so a core path containing a space yields a hook that fails on every commit. Fix: shlex.quote the script path.
close_reason: shlex.quote the script path inside the pre-commit entry string; test covers space-containing path round-trip and that plain paths stay unquoted (dogfood config semantics unchanged — live hooks-check still passes).
